title: A ground working apparatus
abstract: A ground working apparatus with attaches to a hand held drilling machine is provided. The apparatus has a rotatable shaft and a rotatable stabilising tip, blades and tines. The apparatus produces a cutting and digging action which enables the user to perform a wide variety of tasks both above the ground and below the ground.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A ground working apparatus comprising: â¢ an elongated rotatable shaft having a first end and a second distal end, the first end is adapted to releasably engage with a chuck of a drill; Â· at least one cutting blade secured to the shaft; â¢ at least one tine secured to the shaft; and â¢ a stabilising tip at the distal end of the shaft, the tip being adapted to minimise lateral movement of the apparatus relative to the ground when in use; the apparatus formed such that when in use the first end of the shaft is inserted into the chuck of a drill and activation of the drill causes the shaft, blade, tine and tip to rotate resulting in the apparatus being able to penetrate the ground. 2. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 wherein the blade and the tine are secured at or close to the distal end of the shaft. 3. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the blade forms part of a blade disc. 4. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the tine forms part of a tine disc. 5. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the blade forms part of a blade disc and wherein the shaft has a threaded section and the blade is fastened to the threaded section of the shaft. 6. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the tine forms part of a tine disc and wherein the shaft has a threaded section and the tine is fastened to the threaded section of the shaft. 7. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 wherein the stabilising tip is the distal end of the shaft. 8. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the stabilising tip is adapted to releasably engage with the distal end the shaft. 9. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the leading edge of the at least one blade is angled such that it is pitched downwards towards the ground when in use. 10. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the tine is attached to the blade. 11. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the shaft, blade, tine and tip are all made substantially of corrosion resistant material. 12. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the shaft is adjustable in height. 13. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 wherein there are at least four blades. 14. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein there are at least two tines. 15. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the at least one blade or blades are substantially perpendicular to the shaft. 16. A ground working apparatus according to claim 1 wherein the at least one tine is substantially parallel to the shaft and protrude towards the ground when in use. 17. A ground working apparatus according to claim , wherein the tip is tapered. 18. A ground working apparatus according to any claim 1 , wherein the drill is a battery powered hand held drill. 19. A kit of parts suitable for forming an apparatus according to claim 1 , the kit comprising the shaft, the blade, the tine and the tip.
